title: Counterfactual rejoin-window features
summary: Official F1 strategy pieces repeatedly frame pit timing around whether a stop opens a safe rejoin gap, avoids traffic, or covers an undercut.
rationale: Official F1 strategy pieces repeatedly frame pit timing around whether a stop opens a safe rejoin gap, avoids traffic, or covers an undercut. Your current features model tyre wear well but do not explicitly encode the counterfactual: ‘if I pit now, do I rejoin into traffic or clear air, and do I emerge near the target rival?’
implementation_hint: Build approximate rejoin features from current position order and race-lap field context: estimated pit-loss bucket by race, expected rejoin position after a stop now, distance to nearest cars ahead/behind in position order, whether current lap is an ‘open window’ lap from prior pitter patterns, and whether pitting now likely covers/attacks a nearby rival. Keep everything fold-local and based only on information available up to the current lap.
expected_effect: Targets the strategic mechanism behind real pit calls, so it may add signal in ambiguous mid-race windows where tyre-age rules alone are insufficient.
risk: The dataset lacks explicit lap gaps, so rejoin estimates are proxy features; over-engineering weak proxies can hurt unless they are few and regularized.
